sourceMovie.title: Chinatown
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Chinatown (1974) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: I Am All Girls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Chinatown clicked for you, I Am All Girls (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Chinatown?
faq.answer: Try I Am All Girls, Jackie Brown, Gangster Squad—each shares DNA with Chinatown while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Chinatown?
faq.answer: Chinatown (1974) is best described as Crime, Drama, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Chinatown part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Jake Gittes Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
